type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Please note that the information provided was calculated using analytical results from Saskatchewan Research Council using tailings samples from Mosaic Potash Colonsay.   All tailings report to the Tailings Management Area which consists of perimeter dykes and sections of French Drains that provide active perimeter containment.  Additionally the tailings management area have also been constructed in areas where the natural geology is dominated by formations of low permeability tills.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Please note that particulate releases are primarily soluble plant nutrients from a potash fertilizer facility.  Calculations were completed using the SRC generated potash industry estimates.  The climate data that was used to calculate the road dust was taken from Environment Canada website for the Watrous, SK station.

type.fr: Commentaire sur l'élimination

comments.comment: This substance is being added to fulfill the Disposal of Tailings and Waste Rock requirement. All tailings report to the Tailings Management Area which consists of perimeter dykes and sections of French drains that provide active perimeter containment. Additionally the Tailings Management Area at Colonsay has been constructed in an area where the natural geology is dominated by formations of low permeability tills.

comments.comment:
The values have differed since the 2012 reporting year due to changes in source testing completed on non-dryer stacks and the addition of new stacks as a results of a recent mine expansion.

Climate data was used to calculate road dust was taken from the Environment Canada website for the Watrous, SK station for all months.

comments.comment:
Please note that the information provided was calculated using analytical results from Saskatchewan Research
Council using tailings samples from Mosaic Potash Colonsay. All tailings report to the Tailings Management Area
which consists of perimeter dykes and sections of French Drains that provide active perimeter containment.
Additionally the tailings management area have also been constructed in areas where the natural geology is
dominated by formations of low permeability tills.
